title: Sentinel UI: Local Access Control (localhost binding, optional auth, CSRF protection)
description: Secure the sentinel's local UI against unauthorized access and common web attacks. By default the UI binds only to 127.0.0.1 (TASK-054). This task adds: optional Basic Auth or local token authentication for the /ui prefix, CSRF token enforcement for any state-changing UI actions (though mutations are kept to a minimum — primarily CLI), restrictive Content Security Policy and anti-embedding headers, and documentation for Kubernetes port-forward access patterns.
objectives: Optional Basic Auth or env-var-based token for /ui/* routes
CSRF token required for any POST mutation routes under /ui
CSP: default-src 'self', X-Frame-Options: DENY, X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff
Referrer-Policy: no-referrer for all UI responses
Kubernetes port-forward documentation for accessing UI remotely
Startup warning if UI exposed on non-loopback interface without auth enabled

steps.title: Implement optional UI authentication middleware
steps.detail: In `sentinel/ui/access_control.py`, create FastAPI middleware `UIAuthMiddleware`. Check env vars at startup: `SENTINEL_UI_AUTH=none|basic|token` (default: `none`). For `basic`: prompt uses `Authorization: Basic <base64(user:pass)>` where user is `sentinel` and pass is value of `SENTINEL_UI_PASSWORD` env var. If `SENTINEL_UI_PASSWORD` not set and auth=basic: fail startup with `ConfigurationError`. For `token`: check `Authorization: Bearer <token>` where token matches `SENTINEL_UI_TOKEN` env var. For auth failures: return `Response(status_code=401, headers={'WWW-Authenticate': 'Basic realm="Sentinel UI"'})`. Apply only to routes under `/ui/` prefix.

steps.title: Implement CSRF protection
steps.detail: CSRF is only needed if UI has any POST/PUT/DELETE routes (mutation actions). Currently, the UI is read-only — all mutations go through sentinelctl CLI. However, add CSRF token infrastructure for future mutation endpoints. Implement using Double-Submit Cookie pattern: on first GET /ui/*, set `csrf_token=<secrets.token_urlsafe(32)>` in a `SameSite=Strict; HttpOnly; Path=/ui` cookie. For any POST under /ui: validate `X-CSRF-Token` header matches cookie value (constant-time compare). If mismatch: return 403. In Jinja2 base.html template: include `<meta name='csrf-token' content='{{ csrf_token }}'>` and inject into any future form submissions via JS.

steps.title: Security headers middleware
steps.detail: In `sentinel/ui/access_control.py`, add `UISecurityHeadersMiddleware` that adds headers to every `/ui/*` response: `Content-Security-Policy: default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; img-src 'self' data:; connect-src 'self'; frame-ancestors 'none'`. `X-Frame-Options: DENY`. `X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff`. `Referrer-Policy: no-referrer`. `Permissions-Policy: camera=(), microphone=(), geolocation=()`. Apply these headers even on 401/403 responses to prevent clickjacking on the login prompt.

steps.title: Non-loopback exposure warning
steps.detail: In `sentinel/core/startup.py` (extends TASK-054), after binding decision: if `SENTINEL_UI_HOST != '127.0.0.1'` AND `SENTINEL_UI_AUTH == 'none'`: log CRITICAL: `event=ui_insecure_exposure host={host} auth=none. Set SENTINEL_UI_AUTH=token and SENTINEL_UI_TOKEN to protect the UI.`. Do NOT prevent startup, but increment an `insecure_config_warnings_total` Prometheus counter. This allows the operator to consciously override for controlled environments (e.g., Docker Compose dev) while being alerted in monitoring.

steps.title: Kubernetes port-forward documentation
steps.detail: File: `docs/deployment/ui_access.md`. Contents: (1) Default: UI on 127.0.0.1 inside pod, not exposed via Service. (2) Access command: `kubectl port-forward pod/<sentinel-pod> 8081:8080 -n <namespace>` then open `http://127.0.0.1:8081/ui/health`. (3) For automated monitoring: use `kubectl exec` to call `/health/ready` endpoint directly. (4) Security warning: never expose /ui via an Ingress or NodePort in production. (5) If local token auth enabled: set `SENTINEL_UI_TOKEN` via Kubernetes Secret and mount as env var. (6) Session security: close browser after use; UI has no session expiry (timeout in future scope).

steps.title: Tests
steps.detail: Test file: `tests/unit/ui/test_access_control.py`. Cases: (1) GET /ui/health with no auth (auth=none) returns 200; (2) GET /ui/health with auth=basic, valid credentials returns 200; (3) GET /ui/health with auth=basic, wrong password returns 401 with WWW-Authenticate header; (4) GET /ui/health with auth=token, correct token returns 200; (5) GET /ui/health with auth=token, wrong token returns 401; (6) All /ui/* responses have X-Frame-Options: DENY; (7) CSP header present on all /ui/* responses; (8) Non-loopback + auth=none logs CRITICAL warning on startup; (9) CSRF cookie set on first GET /ui/; (10) POST to /ui with missing X-CSRF-Token returns 403.

env_vars.SENTINEL_UI_PASSWORD: password for basic auth (required if auth=basic)
env_vars.SENTINEL_UI_TOKEN: bearer token for token auth (required if auth=token)

security_constraints: If auth=basic and SENTINEL_UI_PASSWORD not set: startup fails (no plaintext default password)
CSRF cookie must be SameSite=Strict to prevent cross-site request inclusion
UI password must not be logged at startup (log auth_mode but not credential value)
Content-Security-Policy frame-ancestors: none prevents embedding in iframes (clickjacking)
Referrer-Policy: no-referrer prevents sensitive path disclosure to external resources (if future UI has external links)
acceptance_criteria: All /ui/* endpoints protected when auth enabled (401 on missing/wrong credentials)
Security headers present on all UI responses including error pages
CSRF cookie set on first visit and required for POST actions
Startup logs CRITICAL when UI exposed on non-loopback without auth
port-forward documentation is accurate and tested manually
